title: Transportation Security Screeners
description: Conduct screening of passengers, baggage, or cargo to ensure compliance with Transportation Security Administration (TSA) regulations. May operate basic security equipment such as x-ray machines and hand wands at screening checkpoints.

tasks.text: Inspect carry-on items, using x-ray viewing equipment, to determine whether items contain objects that warrant further investigation.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Search carry-on or checked baggage by hand when it is suspected to contain prohibited items such as weapons.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Check passengers' tickets to ensure that they are valid, and to determine whether passengers have designations that require special handling, such as providing photo identification.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Test baggage for any explosive materials, using equipment such as explosive detection machines or chemical swab systems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Perform pat-down or hand-held wand searches of passengers who have triggered machine alarms, who are unable to pass through metal detectors, or who have been randomly identified for such searches.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Notify supervisors or other appropriate personnel when security breaches occur.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Send checked baggage through automated screening machines, and set bags aside for searching or rescreening as indicated by equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Decide whether baggage that triggers alarms should be searched or should be allowed to pass through.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Follow those who breach security until police or other security personnel arrive to apprehend them.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Inform other screeners when baggage should not be opened because it might contain explosives.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect checked baggage for signs of tampering.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Ask passengers to remove shoes and divest themselves of metal objects prior to walking through metal detectors.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Close entry areas following security breaches or reopen areas after receiving notification that the airport is secure.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Challenge suspicious people, requesting their badges and asking what their business is in a particular areas.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Patrol work areas to detect any suspicious items.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Contact police directly in cases of urgent security issues, using phones or two-way radios.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Record information about any baggage that sets off alarms in monitoring equipment.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Watch for potentially dangerous persons whose pictures are posted at checkpoints.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Contact leads or supervisors to discuss objects of concern that are not on prohibited object lists.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Confiscate dangerous items and hazardous materials found in opened bags and turn them over to airlines for disposal.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Monitor passenger flow through screening checkpoints to ensure order and efficiency.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Inform passengers of how to mail prohibited items to themselves, or confiscate these items.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Provide directions and respond to passenger inquiries.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Direct passengers to areas where they can pick up their baggage after screening is complete.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: View images of checked bags and cargo, using remote screening equipment, and alert baggage screeners or handlers to any possible problems.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Locate suspicious bags pictured in printouts sent from remote monitoring areas, and set these bags aside for inspection.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.
